content.claim: Hungary's 6- and 8-year gimnázium tracks create selection gates at ages ~10 and ~12 within a system where institutional structures and educational programme structures are not aligned, producing a coordination problem for any reform involving multiple entry points.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Implementation planning lens
limitations: Based on structural observation rather than causal experimental design
Does not quantify the magnitude of coordination costs across entry gates
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
